_description: Reference: a designable login brick from the Authentication marketplace category. Renders ONE <spideriq-auth> custom element with a CLOSED shadow DOM (the password field is never readable from the host page). Pair it with auth-forgot-password and auth-reset-password for the full flow. NOTE: this is the page-block config you POST when inserting the component into a page — NOT a component-create body (the 3 auth components are global, already in the marketplace under category=authentication).
_status: Live end to end. auth_target=dashboard signs into the SpiderIQ dashboard (one-time code -> host-only handoff -> first-party session; no shared cross-domain cookies); auth_target=site_members signs into the site's own members. A placed brick renders, themes, AND signs in.

auth_target.description: REQUIRED on every auth component. Picks the identity system. 'dashboard' = sign into the SpiderIQ dashboard. 'site_members' = sign into the site's own members area. The two never share a user store or session, so decide before you insert — it can't be swapped after sign-in.

_other_props.api_base: SpiderIQ API base (default https://spideriq.ai). Used when auth_target=dashboard.
_other_props.members_base: Per-site members origin. Used when auth_target=site_members.
_other_props.methods: Array of sign-in methods: email_password | google | github | magic_link.
_other_props.theme: CSS-variable overrides applied INSIDE the closed shadow root: primary_color, bg, text, button_radius, font.
_other_props.forgot_link: Page ref / URL to the forgot-password page (login only).
_other_props.redirect_after: Page ref / URL to send the user to after a successful login (login only).

_anti_patterns: Do NOT omit auth_target — it is REQUIRED; the component cannot guess which identity system to use.

Do NOT assert on the visible field labels or button text to verify the form rendered — <spideriq-auth> uses a CLOSED shadow DOM, so the host page can't read inside it. Verify by checking the page's shadow_hosts include 'spideriq-auth'.
Do NOT initiate OAuth on the site's own domain when auth_target=dashboard — google/github always redirect to the central SpiderIQ origin for the OAuth dance.

Do NOT hand-roll a custom auth form — embed the <spideriq-auth> brick into your own page design; it keeps the password in a closed shadow root and wires the secure sign-in flow. Only the form interior is themed via theme tokens.
